commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Let their eyes be darkened, that they see not; and make their loins continually to shake. The Babylonians were ordered by Cyrus to look upon the Persians as their masters. (Xenophon vii. ) Nothing could more strikingly point out the present condition of the Jews than this passage. They are every where kept under, and see not the sense of the Scriptures, (Calmet) and the truth of Christ's doctrine; but are bent on worldly gain. (Worthington) (2 Corinthians iii. ) (Menochius).
